verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (/loop). A denoise R-rule (R14: `invalidateOrgSlugCache` extraction from iter-011 F2) was promoted but the original F2 proof test only covered 2 of the 3 in-repo call sites (content.ts + members.ts). The third inline block in workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts was filed as a follow-up bead. This iter resolves it: replaced the inline block with the helper, extended the existing F2 proof test SITES array to include settings.ts, both pass.

route:self:promoted-helper-missed-call-s.notes: Fingerprint: a promoted denoise rule extracted a helper, but the proof test only covered N-1 known sites; an Nth site filed as a separate bead lands as rung-1 (mechanical apply of the same recipe). If 3+ similar beads land, rule candidate: 'when a denoise R-rule promotes, the cycle that promotes the rule SHOULD enumerate ALL in-repo sites and either fix all or file beads against all of them'. Codex-y6x9j was originally misclassified rung-4 in iter-002 because the description text mentioned `cache` and the classifier conservatively matched a `.env`-touching path — re-read of the description showed it's a pure mechanical helper-extract.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: iter-002 classifier rung-4'd this bead because the description string mentioned `.env`/cache patterns. iter-003 brief flagged the suspect classification, the agent re-read and re-classified rung-1, and the fix landed in <12 minutes. Suggests classifier should sample-read the cited file:line before sending a bead to rung-4 on keyword match alone.

signal:misclassification-keyword-false-p.notes: Meta-signal: tracks how often the classifier's regex/keyword filter mistakes a description for a high-impact path when the actual cited file is in a different package. If 3+ recurrences, promote rule: 'classifier MUST read the cited file:line for any bead being routed to rung-4 on path-keyword match alone — false-positive cost is low (one extra read) and false-negative cost is high (real fixes deferred indefinitely)'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Remote functions are the planned architecture, not dead code. If the forms aren't wired up but exist by intent, /backend-dev should wire them properly + fix the typo so reset-password and forgot-password functionality works as designed.

route:pending:codex-ttavz-12-auth-remote.notes: Routed to /backend-dev on iter-001. User rejected the 'delete orphan' default in favor of 'wire up planned functionality' interpretation. Pattern fingerprint anchors on denoise:web-auth-remote-broken-endpoint. Ledger schema corrected this iter: rung_density should be a count of beads matching the fingerprint per iter, not the rung number.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: First AskUserQuestion render returned 'im going to need more info on the task whats the issue'. Question payload had options + descriptions + denoise labels + blast-radius context, but no code excerpts. Parent supplied the missing context manually before re-rendering. If this recurs 3+ times, promote rule: rung-3 payloads MUST embed the relevant diff/code excerpts inline.

signal:underspecified-payload-rung-3.notes: Meta-pattern: tracks how often the rung-3 payload format itself underspecifies the question. Distinct from a routing pattern — this is a skill-self-improvement signal.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto mode (/loop). Pure mechanical path correction across 5 sibling test files; bead self-described as 'no proof needed (will fail loud)' — proof shape applied was filesystem-existence check that the corrected repoRoot resolves to a real package.json + apps/web.

route:self:proof-test-path-mechanical-fi.notes: Fingerprint: rung-1 mechanical fix on denoise proof-test infrastructure (no denoise:* label on the bead, but the touched files are __denoise_proofs__/iter-NNN/). If 3+ similar beads land, rule candidate: 'beads describing path/import/byte-mechanical fixes in __denoise_proofs__/ are auto-rung-1 even when their parent fingerprint is denoise-shaped, because they are scaffolding fixes not finding-resolutions'.

verdict_history.user_reasoning: Auto-loop policy this cycle: skip rung 2+ beads, only resolve rung 0/1. Eligible queue had 28 beads — only 1 classified as rung 1 after applying R7 (epic→4), R8 (no file:line→3), and high-impact path filter. Indicates the open backlog is dominated by reasoned/design-shaped work that genuinely needs a human.

Auto-loop policy continues. iter-003 succeeded only because the brief explicitly flagged a misclassification (Codex-y6x9j) for re-examination; without that hint the cycle would have returned `{ok: false, no rung 0/1 beads available}`. Reinforces signal that auto-loop /triage is sustainable only as long as misclassified rung-1 candidates remain hidden in the rung-4 list.

signal:auto-loop-skip-rung-2-plus.notes: Hits=2 of 3 needed for promotion. If iter-004 also exits with ≤1 rung-1 candidate, promote rule: 'after 3 consecutive auto-loop /triage cycles where rung-1 yield ≤1 per cycle, parent should pause /loop and ask user to greenlight rung-3/4 work or switch to a different skill'. The iter-003 verdict also adds a sub-pattern: relying on per-cycle classifier hints to surface misclassified beads is unsustainable — the classifier itself needs the upgrade tracked under signal:misclassification-keyword-false-positive.
